description: Make `npm run start:mvp` run a dedicated smoke entrypoint that boots the composed app, simulates one trusted-admin message, auto-resumes the newly created approval, and reports the final thread status without changing the reusable CLI contract.

userStories.title: Add a dedicated smoke runner for the approval loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated `runMvpSmoke(...)` entrypoint so that the repository has a deterministic one-command smoke path that follows the real bootstrap, gateway, and task-service composition.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/smoke.ts` exports `runMvpSmoke(...)` with injectable bootstrap and streams
The smoke runner snapshots existing approval IDs before sending the scripted inbound message
The smoke runner detects the new pending approval from the current run, resumes it, and prints the final thread status
The smoke runner exits non-zero when no new pending approval is created or no final thread status can be resolved

userStories.title: Keep the operator CLI contract unchanged
userStories.description: As a local operator, I want the existing CLI command surface to keep its current behavior so that adding the smoke entrypoint does not break reusable `message`, `approve`, `reject`, or `tui` flows.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/cli.ts` remains unchanged by the smoke-entrypoint slice
CLI still prints usage for missing or unknown subcommands
The smoke file owns its own `main()` wrapper instead of routing through the CLI

userStories.title: Repoint `start:mvp` and document the smoke contract
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `start:mvp` and the repository docs to describe the real smoke entrypoint so that the documented startup behavior matches what the command actually does.
acceptanceCriteria: `README.md` describes the dedicated smoke runner and its success and failure contract
`docs/plans/README.md` keeps the active design and implementation plan visible during the slice
`npm run start:mvp` no longer prints CLI usage on the default path

userStories.title: Verify and track the dedicated smoke-entrypoint slice
userStories.description: As a developer, I want focused verification, full verification, runtime contract validation, and refreshed Ralph tracking so that the branch state matches the implemented `start:mvp` behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: The previous active Ralph run is archived before reusing `ralph/prd.json` and `ralph/progress.txt`
